Gold NSW Tourism Award
Welcome Back · 2026 Season

Welcome, and Welcome Back!

So much happens in a Bash year, and in the blink of an eye, you're a year older and wiser, and doing it all again. To our returning crew, welcome to another Bash year, and to our 2026 newbies, we're excited to welcome you to our fantastic team; we hope you'll get swept up in the adventure of it all.

There's nothing like camping out in the desert under a blanket of stars, working hard in the fresh air, coming together at night to fill up on hearty tucker that you didn't have to cook (unless you're one of our fabulous caterers, of course) and sitting around the fire, chatting about the events of the day.

Long-term crew say the Bashes are a bit like school camp (in a good way, of course). You come along feeling a bit uncertain and by the end of an intense few weeks, and then over a few years, you get to know each other so well, you almost feel like family (also in a good way).

News & Updates

Big Red Bash

Big Red Bash 2026 — Cancelled

By now, you would have all heard the news of the Big Red Bash cancellation. It was a sad day in the OMFG office when Greg and the team reluctantly concluded that the show just couldn't go on this year. Bloody weather eh? On the plus side, we've had overwhelmingly positive feedback from patrons, vollies and crew about the decision. They absolutely get it.

We're thrilled that a huge majority of volunteers have elected to roll over their shifts until 2027 (how could they possibly say no to the very persuasive, Shenelle), and a huge number of patrons have opted to take up tickets for 2027 in lieu of a refund. We plan to put on the best Big Red Bash ever to make up for two years of Birdsville withdrawal symptoms.

Happily, we still have Mundi to look forward to — and mostly it'll be Mundi as you know it, but with a few tweaks and surprises.

New for this season

What's Different in 2026

01

South Plaza Arts Hub

The South plaza is becoming an arts hub with great workshops and activities for patrons — a whole new vibe in that corner of Mundiville.

02

New Shade Domes

The plaza will feature new shade domes, which we anticipate will be very welcome on those hot August days. You're welcome, patrons.

03

NRMA Major Sponsor

We're welcoming NRMA on board as a major sponsor for this year. A great partnership for the Bash and for the Outback community.

04

Mundi Mundi Lightfest

2026 introduces the Mundi Mundi Lightfest — a lead-in event in Broken Hill. If you've still got some oomph left in you after work, you're welcome to head in for a look!

Coming soon: There's a great new patron activity in the planning stages too, but if we told you… well, you know what we'd have to do!

Farewell, Maggie McKay

You might have noticed that Maggie isn't on the Blackheath list. That's because she's winding up her work with OMFG and will be leaving us in the next few weeks. We'd love to tell you what her next adventure will be, but at the moment, even she doesn't know, other than a hiking trip to Japan with some long days on the trail to think about her next move.

Maggie will be handing over all her crew lists to Shenelle, so after the next few weeks, Shenelle will be the person to call or email if you have questions about your Mundi work, and she'll be busy keeping you in the loop with anything you need to know. For the newbies, do ask for Shenelle when you get to site, and she'll show you around and help you settle in.

Your new contact Shenelle Host shenelle@omfgroup.com.au

19 Aug Pre-event Concert
20–22 Aug Main Event Dates
25 Aug Last Staff Leave Site
How we communicate

Staying in Touch

Facebook

We host the Bash Crew Group on Facebook, which is where we post any news, changes etc, and tips and tricks for a safe and happy Bash experience. If you're not already on the group, please drop Shenelle a line so she can invite you. And make sure you've pinned the group at the top of your feed, because, as Aerosmith suggests, you don't wanna miss a thing.

Email

If you're not on Facebook, don't worry. We'll make sure you hear the news by email. We'll also email you with individual queries, and you should keep an eye on your inbox for your job offer and site safety induction. Do check your junk mail just in case.

WhatsApp

Please download Whatsapp before you get to the Bash site (or Mundiville, as it's officially known). We use Whatsapp to keep in touch on site, along with crew meetings and the notice board. Don't worry though. We've taken your 2025 feedback on board and we'll do less 'all crew' messaging and make better use of Whatsapp for individual team comms.

NSW Tourism & Events Awards 2025

Gold, gold, gold for Mundi 🏆

NSW Tourism & Events Awards — Gold!

In case we didn't tell you, and honestly, we're pretty sure we shouted it from the rooftops, the Mundi Mundi Bash won gold in the Major Festivals and Events Category of the NSW Tourism and Events awards for 2025. Your management team had an absolute blast, swapping our work pants and hi-vis for sequins and bow ties. Here we are:

With your help, we hope to be in the running for gold at the 2026 Qantas Australian Tourism Awards.

Stay Safe at Mundiville

Safety Tip

Know How to Get Help

Make sure you know the location of the Event Office and Control Room on site. Save Shenelle's number in your phone before you arrive. If you see something that doesn't look right — report it. Your safety and the safety of those around you is our top priority.

Country, Community & Culture

Cool Stuff to Know

Wilyakali Country

The traditional owners of the Bash site are the Wilyakali people. Their traditional lands encompass about 22,000 km² from the Barrier Ranges westwards to Olary in South Australia, with close ties to the Barka/Baaka (Darling River).

We host a couple of Wilyakali stalls at Mundi Mundi — do drop in and introduce yourself as you move around the plaza. You'll also hear the traditional owners welcome us to their country from the main stage.

Uncle Glenn O'Donnell — one of the applicants in the successful Wilyakali Native Title Claim — will be featured at the event.

Belmont Station

The leaseholders of the Bash site, Belmont Station, are the Blore Family — John, Airlie and their kids. John has lived at Belmont Station for 26 years. The family's business, Silverton Goats, sources and transports rangeland goats. The Silverton Wind Farm turbines are the first thing you'll see when you arrive on site.

Silverton

Just 14km down the road is the historic town of Silverton — home to the iconic Silverton Hotel, Daydream Mine, Silverton Gaol & Historical Museum, art galleries, and the Mundi Mundi lookout that gained worldwide fame through the Mad Max movies. Some of Furiosa was filmed on Belmont Station, and the Mad Max Museum in town is dedicated to the franchise.

BROKEN HILL MUNDI MUNDI BASH Rockin' the Plains · Outback NSW
